title: Surviving the Night Without Gear
immediate_steps: Stop moving at least 2 hours before dark
Prioritize finding shelter from the wind (a rock overhang, a fallen tree, or dense evergreens)
Insulate your body from the cold ground using thick layers of dry leaves or pine boughs
Stuff your clothing with dry leaves or grass for insulation
Huddle in a fetal position to conserve body heat
Stay awake and do isometric exercises (flexing muscles) to generate heat if you are shivering uncontrollably
avoid: Do not attempt to hike in the dark (high risk of injury)
Do not lie directly on the cold ground
Do not sleep if you are severely hypothermic and alone
